//: Example wiring for ~/.claude/settings.json. Merge the blocks you want. Hook order within a matcher does not affect safety — an exit-2 block from ANY hook always wins, and a hook 'allow' never overrides a settings deny rule. The bash-guards are OPINIONATED (worktree workflow + personal bash style); include only the ones you want.

permissions.//allow-test: Test-runner conveniences. `Bash(pytest:*)` auto-approves pytest (interpreter-trust, like python3 below — it runs your test code by design). Shell suites named `*.test.sh` are auto-run by the approve-test-run.sh hook below (a STRICT `bash <script>.test.sh` parse — never `bash -c …` / env-prefix / extra write segment), which is paired with enforce-test-location.sh keeping those files under a tests/ dir inside ~/.claude. NO `Bash(bash …)` allow rule is used: a `bash *.test.sh` glob would let `bash -c '<payload>' x.test.sh` smuggle arbitrary code through a single segment.
permissions.//allow-generic: OPTIONAL prompt-reducing baseline — keep only the rules you actually want; each is a CONSCIOUS opt-in. These are COARSE PREFIX matches, so they auto-approve MORE than their happy-path name suggests, and the permissions.deny block below only gates a few well-known footguns (git push --force, npm publish, curl|sh) — it is NOT a complete backstop. Specifically: Bash(git:*) also auto-approves destructive git (reset --hard, clean -fdx), a branch switch (git checkout <branch>, which this kit's own git-branch-switch-guard otherwise blocks), and git-config-based arbitrary execution (git -c core.pager=<cmd> / core.sshCommand=<cmd>); Bash(npm:*)/Bash(npx:*) run arbitrary package lifecycle scripts (preinstall/postinstall) = arbitrary code. If that surface matters to you, drop git/npm here and allow only read-only subcommands. The build/lang allows cover the SAME bare names as hooks/bash-guards/bare-interpreter-guard.sh, so the guard's rewrite of an absolute interpreter path always lands on an approved command. auto-approve-readonly.sh already covers pure read-only commands; these add the WRITE-capable tools it deliberately defers.

PreToolUse.//: enforce-test-location pairs with approve-test-run above: since that hook auto-runs any *.test.sh, this keeps *.test.sh files under a tests/ dir inside ~/.claude (fail-open; ~/tmp and other projects exempt). Gates Write AND Edit.
